LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, FEB-26-2019 HEARING HELD ON PLAINTIFF ECOLOGICAL RIGHTS FOUNDATION'S MOTION TO APPROVE PROPOSITION 65 SETTLEMENT AND FOR ENTRY OF CONSENT JUDGMENT AS TO DEFENDANT KAY PARK-REC CORPORATION. THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF ECOLOGICAL RIGHTS FOUNDATION'S MOTION FOR APPROVAL OF THE SETTLEMENT IN THIS ACTION AS TO DEFENDANT KAY PARK-REC CORPORATION AND FOR ENTRY OF THE [PROPOSED] CONSENT JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED AND THE SETTLEMENT MEETS THE REQUIREMENTS OF PROPOSITION 65. (HEALTH & SAFETY CODE 25249.7(F)(4).) MOVING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER AND PROPOSED CONSENT JUDGMENT THAT IS VERBATIM WITH THE COURT'S RULING. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. (302/EPS)

THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: (CONSENT JUDGMENT AS TO GIBSON OVERSEAS, INC. (SEE SCANNED DOCUMENT)

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF ECOLOGICAL RIGHTS FOUNDATION'S MOTION TO APPROVE PROPOSITION 65 SETTLEMENT AND FOR ENTRY OF CONSENT JUDGMENT AS TO DEFENDANT GIBSON OVERSEAS, INC. IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED AND THE SETTLEMENT COMPLIES WITH THE REQUIREMENTS FOR PROPOSITION 65 SETTLEMENTS UNDER HEALTH & SAFETY CODE SECTION 25249.7. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE WARNING THAT IS REQUIRED BY THE SETTLEMENT COMPLIES WITH HEALTH & SAFETY CODE SECTIONS 25249.5-25249.14; THE AWARD OF ATTORNEY'S FEES IS REASONABLE UNDER CALIFORNIA LAW; AND THE PENALTY AMOUNT IS REASONABLE BASED ON THE CRITERIA SET FORTH IN HEALTH & SAFETY CODE SECTION 25249.7(B)(2). THE COURT WILL EXECUTE THE PROPOSED ORDER AND CONSENT JUDGMENT INCLUDED WITH THE MOVING PAPERS. ORDER AND JUDGEMENT ARE SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER AND JUDGMENT FOR DETAILS) JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, NOT REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Sep-24-2019 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And For Entry Of Consent Judgment As To Defendant R.J. Thomas Manufacturing Company, Inc.
Off Calendar Aug-19-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

RULING: There being no appearance, the tentative ruling is adopted on PLAINTIFF ECOLOGICAL RIGHTS FOUNDATION'S Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And For Entry Of Consent Judgment As To Defendant R.J. Thomas Manufacturing Company, Inc., as follows:

Plaintiff Ecological Rights Foundation's motion to approve Proposition 65 settlement and for entry of consent judgment (R.J. Thomas Manufacturing Company, Inc.) is GRANTED. No opposition filed and the settlement complies with the requirements for Proposition 65 settlements under Health & Safety Code section 25249.7. The Court finds that the warning that is required by the settlement complies with Health & Safety Code sections 25249.5-25249.14; the award of attorney's fees is reasonable under California law; and the penalty amount is reasonable based on the criteria set forth in Health & Safety Code section 25249.7(b)(2).

The Court signed the proposed order and consent judgment included with the moving papers.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And For Entry Of Consent Judgment As To Defendant Gibson Overseas, Inc.
Off Calendar Apr-11-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF ECOLOGICAL RIGHTS FOUNDATION'S Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And For Entry Of Consent Judgment As To Defendant Gibson Overseas, Inc.

laintiff Ecological Rights Foundation's motion to approve Proposition 65 settlement and for entry of consent judgment as to Defendant Gibson Overseas, Inc. is granted. No opposition filed and the settlement complies with the requirements for Proposition 65 settlements under Health & Safety Code section 25249.7. The Court finds that the warning that is required by the settlement complies with Health & Safety Code sections 25249.5-25249.14; the award of attorney's fees is reasonable under California law; and the penalty amount is reasonable based on the criteria set forth in Health & Safety Code section 25249.7(b)(2). The Court will execute the proposed order and consent judgment included with the moving papers.

Order and Judgement are signed. (see scanned order and judgment for details)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And For Entry Of Consent Judgment (Kay Park-Rec Corporation)

MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF ECOLOGICAL RIGHTS FOUNDATION's Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And For Entry Of Consent Judgment as to Defendant Kay Park-Rec Corporation.

Plaintiff Ecological Rights Foundation's motion for approval of the settlement in this action as to Defendant Kay Park-Rec Corporation and for entry of the [Proposed] Consent Judgment is granted. No opposition filed and the settlement meets the requirements of Proposition 65. (Health & Safety Code 25249.7(f)(4).)

Moving party to submit a proposed order and proposed consent judgment that is verbatim with the Court's ruling.
